models.name: Llama 3.2 (3B)
models.description: Meta's highly efficient 3B model. Extremely fast and perfectly suited for low-end laptops and edge devices.

models.name: Phi 3.5 (3.8B)
models.description: Microsoft's tiny but mighty reasoning model. Punches way above its weight class for logical tasks.

models.name: Gemma 2 (2B)
models.description: Google's 2B parameter model built on the Gemini architecture. Extremely capable for its tiny footprint.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 (3B)
models.description: Alibaba's 3B model. Outstanding performance in mathematics and reasoning for budget setups.

models.name: TinyLlama (1.1B)
models.description: A 1.1B LLaMA model that can run literally anywhere. Great for basic text generation on ancient hardware.

models.name: Dolphin Phi (2.7B)
models.description: An uncensored, highly compliant fine-tune of Microsoft's Phi. Great for strict system prompt adherence.

models.name: Llama 3.1 (8B)
models.description: The gold standard for local LLMs. Meta's 8B parameter model is highly capable and runs perfectly on 8GB VRAM.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 (7B)
models.description: One of the best 7B models currently available. Excellent world knowledge and multilingual support.

models.name: Gemma 2 (9B)
models.description: Google's 9B model. Phenomenal reasoning capabilities that rival models twice its size.

models.name: Mistral (7B)
models.description: The legendary 7B model from Mistral AI. Fast, unrestrictive, and excellent for creative writing.

models.name: OpenHermes 2.5 (7B)
models.description: A very popular community fine-tune of Mistral. Highly conversational and engaging.

models.name: Dolphin Mistral (7B)
models.description: An uncensored fine-tune of Mistral. Stripped of alignment, making it extremely obedient to instructions.

models.name: Neural Chat (7B)
models.description: Intel's fine-tuned model optimized for excellent general-purpose dialogue.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 (14B)
models.description: A robust 14B model balancing speed and deep reasoning capability. Requires a solid GPU.

models.name: Mixtral (8x7B)
models.description: Mistral's Mixture-of-Experts model. Extremely intelligent, but requires significant RAM.

models.name: Command R (35B)
models.description: Cohere's 35B model specifically optimized for RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) workflows.

models.name: Llama 3.1 (70B)
models.description: Meta's flagship open-source model. Rivals GPT-4 in capability. Requires dual GPUs or massive RAM.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 (72B)
models.description: Alibaba's massive flagship model. Exceptionally smart, dominating local leaderboards.

models.name: Command R+ (104B)
models.description: Cohere's massive 104B parameter powerhouse. Incredible reasoning, but incredibly heavy to run.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 Coder (7B)
models.description: The best 7B coding model on the market. Excels in Python, JS, and C++.

models.name: DeepSeek Coder (6.7B)
models.description: A highly capable 6.7B model trained entirely on code. Excellent for IDE integrations.

models.name: CodeLlama (7B)
models.description: Meta's dedicated coding fine-tune of the LLaMA architecture.

models.name: Phi 3 (14B)
models.description: Microsoft's 14B model. Incredibly smart at logic and algorithm development.

models.name: DeepSeek Coder V2 (16B)
models.description: DeepSeek's advanced MoE coding model. Highly intelligent and context-aware.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 Coder (32B)
models.description: Alibaba's flagship 32B coding AI. Rivals commercial models for raw programming ability.

models.name: CodeGemma (7B)
models.description: Google's lightweight coding model based on Gemma. Fast and very efficient.

models.name: StarCoder 2 (7B)
models.description: Trained on the Stack v2 dataset. Excellent multi-language support.

models.name: LLaVA (7B)
models.description: Large Language-and-Vision Assistant. Can describe images and answer questions about photos.

models.name: BakLLaVA (7B)
models.description: Mistral-based vision model. Excellent at OCR and extracting text from images.

models.name: Moondream (1.8B)
models.description: Extremely tiny, highly optimized vision model. Analyzes images quickly on budget hardware.

models.name: Llama 3.2 (1B)
models.description: Meta's ultra-compact 1B model. Runs on virtually any device including Raspberry Pi. Ideal for simple Q&A, summarization, and edge deployments.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 (0.5B)
models.description: Alibaba's tiniest model at just 500M parameters. Blazing fast and fits in under 1GB of RAM. Perfect for keyword extraction and ultra-lightweight on-device inference.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 (1.5B)
models.description: Alibaba's 1.5B model. Surprisingly capable for its size — great for text classification, translation snippets, and basic chat on very limited hardware.

models.name: SmolLM2 (135M)
models.description: Hugging Face's 135M micro-model. The lightest useful LLM in existence. Ideal for on-device inference on IoT gadgets and embedded systems where even 1B is too large.

models.name: SmolLM2 (360M)
models.description: Hugging Face's 360M compact model. Trained on a new high-quality dataset for better instruction following at this tiny scale. Perfect for resource-constrained chatbots.

models.name: SmolLM2 (1.7B)
models.description: The largest SmolLM2 model from Hugging Face. Excellent reasoning for its size, strong instruction following, and trained on a curated high-quality corpus. A great 1B-class choice.

models.name: Gemma 3 (1B)
models.description: Google's newest 1B Gemma model. Text-only with a 32K context window. Fastest local model in the Gemma 3 lineup — great for testing, rapid prototyping, and low-power devices.

models.name: Gemma 3 (4B)
models.description: Google's 4B Gemma 3 model. Punches well above its weight class with multimodal vision support and a 128K context window. Top pick for constrained hardware in 2025.

models.name: Gemma 3 (12B)
models.description: Google's capable 12B Gemma 3 model. Strong instruction following, vision support, and multilingual ability in a compact form. Rivals 20B+ models on many benchmarks.

models.name: Gemma 3 (27B)
models.description: Google's flagship Gemma 3 model. Exceptional reasoning, vision understanding, and multilingual support. One of the best single-GPU models available in this size class.

models.name: Phi 4 Mini (3.8B)
models.description: Microsoft's latest small model. A step up from Phi 3.5 with dramatically better reasoning and math. The only viable reasoning model if you're limited to 8GB RAM.

models.name: Phi 4 (14B)
models.description: Microsoft's flagship small model. State-of-the-art on STEM and MATH benchmarks, scoring 80.4% on MATH — beating models 5x its size. Dense knowledge per parameter.

models.name: Mistral Small 3 (24B)
models.description: Mistral's new benchmark-setter for 'small' models under 70B. Near-70B quality at 14GB RAM. One of the best value-for-VRAM models available — highly recommended for mid-range machines.

models.name: Llama 3.3 (70B)
models.description: Meta's new state-of-the-art 70B. Achieves similar performance to the massive 405B model at a fraction of the cost. The best overall open-source model for those with 48GB+ VRAM.

models.name: DeepSeek R1 Distill (1.5B)
models.description: Distilled from DeepSeek R1's reasoning traces into a Qwen 2.5 1.5B base. Surpasses OpenAI o1-preview on math despite its tiny size. Chain-of-thought reasoning at ultra-low hardware cost.

models.name: DeepSeek R1 Distill (7B)
models.description: DeepSeek's chain-of-thought reasoning in a compact 7B form. Think-before-you-answer style makes it the best local math and logic model at this size. Ideal for coding and debugging.

models.name: DeepSeek R1 Distill (8B)
models.description: A Llama 3.1 8B base distilled with DeepSeek R1 reasoning traces. Visible chain-of-thought makes it great for step-by-step problem solving. A fan-favourite for local AI users.

models.name: DeepSeek R1 Distill (14B)
models.description: Mid-tier reasoning powerhouse distilled into a Qwen 2.5 14B base. Balances deep analytical thinking with manageable hardware requirements. Great for complex research tasks.

models.name: DeepSeek R1 Distill (32B)
models.description: The 'sweet spot' reasoning model. Shows its thinking before answering, making it exceptional for debugging complex code and logic puzzles. Matches GPT-4 class on many analytical tasks.

models.name: DeepSeek R1 Distill (70B)
models.description: Top-tier reasoning distilled into a Llama 3.3 70B base. Combines Meta's flagship open model with DeepSeek's chain-of-thought methodology. For serious enthusiast hardware only.

models.name: Qwen 3 (0.6B)
models.description: The smallest Qwen 3 dense model. Next-generation architecture in a sub-1B package. Supports 29+ languages and runs on virtually any hardware including phones.

models.name: Qwen 3 (1.7B)
models.description: Qwen 3's second-smallest dense variant. Strong multilingual and reasoning ability for under 2GB memory. A solid upgrade over TinyLlama for budget deployments.

models.name: Qwen 3 (4B)
models.description: A 4B dense Qwen 3 model with hybrid thinking mode. Supports extended chain-of-thought reasoning on demand. Best-in-class at 4B for multilingual chat and analysis.

models.name: Qwen 3 (8B)
models.description: Latest-gen 8B from Alibaba's Qwen3 series. Strong multilingual support across 29+ languages, hybrid thinking mode, and 128K context. Outperforms Llama 3.1 8B on most benchmarks.

models.name: Qwen 3 (14B)
models.description: Qwen's dense 14B model. Hybrid think/non-think mode lets you toggle reasoning depth on the fly. Superb for academic research and complex document analysis.

models.name: Qwen 3 (32B)
models.description: Qwen's dense 32B model. Among the smartest models runnable on a single consumer GPU. Excellent coding, math, and multilingual reasoning. The go-to choice for a 24GB VRAM setup.

models.name: Gemma 2 (27B)
models.description: Google's largest Gemma 2 model. Exceptional reasoning and strong multilingual support. Requires a powerful single GPU or M2/M3 MacBook Pro to run smoothly.

models.name: Orca Mini (3B)
models.description: Microsoft Research's efficient 3B instruction-tuned model. Trained on explanation-augmented data for better reasoning. Excellent for old laptops and offline tasks.

models.name: Vicuna (7B)
models.description: UC Berkeley's pioneering fine-tune of LLaMA. Trained on quality ChatGPT conversations. Feels natural and engaging — a classic model that popularized local LLMs.

models.name: Vicuna (13B)
models.description: The larger 13B version of Vicuna. Better for long multi-turn conversations and nuanced discussions. A trusted classic for building personal AI assistants.

models.name: Starling (7B)
models.description: UC Berkeley's Starling LM model trained with RLHF from GPT-4. Remarkably helpful and harmless for its size. Great balance of capability and safety for customer-facing apps.

models.name: Aya (8B)
models.description: Cohere's multilingual model covering 100+ languages. Tuned specifically for cross-lingual dialogue, translation, and cultural nuance. The top pick for non-English conversations.

models.name: OLMo 2 (7B)
models.description: Allen AI's fully open-source model with transparent training data. Trained on up to 5T tokens and competitive with Llama 3.1 on English benchmarks. Great for research reproducibility.

models.name: OLMo 2 (13B)
models.description: Allen AI's 13B fully open research model. Best-in-class among truly open models — training data, code, and weights all public. Ideal for academic and enterprise transparency needs.

models.name: Cogito v1 (8B)
models.description: Deep Cogito's hybrid reasoning model. Outperforms LLaMA, DeepSeek, and Qwen counterparts of the same size on most standard benchmarks. Strong logical deduction and planning capabilities.

models.name: Cogito v1 (14B)
models.description: Deep Cogito's 14B hybrid reasoning model. Combines fast inference with optional extended thinking. Beats comparable Qwen and Llama models on reasoning and instruction benchmarks.

models.name: Mistral NeMo (12B)
models.description: A 12B model jointly built by Mistral AI and NVIDIA. 128K context window and excellent world knowledge. Best in its size class for long-document summarization and enterprise chat.

models.name: Nemotron Mini (4B)
models.description: NVIDIA's compact 4B model built for enterprise chat and function calling. Excellent structured output generation and tool use. A strong edge AI choice for NVIDIA Jetson devices.

models.name: Llama 3.1 (405B)
models.description: Meta's colossal 405B model — the most powerful open-source LLM ever released. Rivals GPT-4o on nearly all tasks. Requires a multi-GPU server or very large unified memory Apple Silicon.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 (32B)
models.description: Alibaba's capable 32B model. Bridges the gap between mid-tier and enthusiast hardware. Excellent for long-context document analysis and professional writing.

models.name: IBM Granite 3 (8B)
models.description: IBM's enterprise-grade 8B model. Designed for business applications, summarization, and RAG. Commercially permissive Apache 2.0 license — safe for production enterprise use.

models.name: Llama 2 (7B)
models.description: Meta's original Llama 2 — the model that kickstarted the local AI revolution. Still widely used for compatibility with older tooling and fine-tuning pipelines.

models.name: Llama 2 (13B)
models.description: Meta's 13B Llama 2 model. A step up from the 7B with noticeably better coherence in long conversations. Good baseline for fine-tuning experiments.

models.name: SOLAR (10.7B)
models.description: Upstage's 10.7B model built with depth-up scaling. Tiny footprint, big brains — one of the best 10B-class models. Great for summarization and instruction following.

models.name: InternLM 2 (7B)
models.description: Shanghai AI Lab's powerful 7B model. Excellent tool calling and code-related tasks. Strong for Chinese-English bilingual applications.

models.name: QwQ (32B)
models.description: Alibaba's experimental deep-reasoning model at 32B. Uses extended chain-of-thought to self-verify answers. Exceptional for math olympiad problems and complex logical analysis.

models.name: Mixtral (8x22B)
models.description: Mistral's large MoE powerhouse with 141B total parameters but only ~39B active per token. Server-class performance. Needs 80GB+ VRAM but delivers near-GPT-4 quality.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 Coder (0.5B)
models.description: Alibaba's tiniest coding model. Designed for lightweight code completion on edge devices. Useful for simple autocomplete in resource-constrained IDEs.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 Coder (1.5B)
models.description: A 1.5B coding-specific model that fits in minimal memory. Excellent for simple script generation, refactoring, and code explanation on low-end hardware.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 Coder (3B)
models.description: Budget-tier coding model with solid Python, JS, and Bash support. A great on-device coding assistant for laptops that can't run 7B+ models.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 Coder (14B)
models.description: Alibaba's 14B coding specialist. Strong on algorithm design, SQL generation, and multi-file refactoring. Requires a solid GPU but delivers professional-grade code output.

models.name: CodeLlama (13B)
models.description: Meta's 13B coding model. Better code generation than the 7B with improved context retention for larger files. Supports 20+ programming languages including Rust and Go.

models.name: CodeLlama (34B)
models.description: Meta's largest CodeLlama. Top-tier code generation for complex debugging and cross-file refactoring. Handles intricate codebases requiring deep context understanding.

models.name: StarCoder 2 (3B)
models.description: Lightweight 3B coding model trained on the Stack v2. Surprisingly capable for its size — great for multi-language code completion on budget hardware.

models.name: StarCoder 2 (15B)
models.description: The most capable StarCoder 2 model. Trained on 600+ programming languages from The Stack v2. Excellent fill-in-the-middle code completion for professional development workflows.

models.name: DeepSeek Coder (1.3B)
models.description: DeepSeek's smallest coding model. Trained on 2 trillion code tokens. Great for lightweight autocomplete and syntax assistance on minimal hardware.

models.name: DeepSeek Coder (33B)
models.description: DeepSeek's large coding model. Exceptional at repository-level code generation and complex algorithm implementation. Knows 87 programming languages.

models.name: CodeGemma (2B)
models.description: Google's ultra-compact coding model based on Gemma architecture. Designed for fill-in-the-middle completion directly in your IDE on budget hardware.

models.name: Phi 4 Reasoning (14B)
models.description: Microsoft's reasoning-focused variant of Phi 4. Uses extended chain-of-thought to exceed DeepSeek-R1 Distill Llama 70B on reasoning benchmarks despite being a 14B model.

models.name: Qwen 3 Coder (30B MoE)
models.description: Alibaba's agentic coding model trained with RL on SWE-Bench. Only 3.3B parameters activate per token via MoE, giving 30B quality at 7B inference speed. Built for multi-file code agents.

models.name: Devstral Small (24B)
models.description: Mistral's coding-specialized model. Apache 2.0 licensed for commercial use. Excellent agentic coding ability for repository-level tasks and automated code review workflows.

models.name: IBM Granite Code (3B)
models.description: IBM's small code model designed for enterprise software development. Apache 2.0 licensed. Trained on permissively licensed code — safe for commercial code generation.

models.name: IBM Granite Code (8B)
models.description: IBM's mid-tier coding model. Trained on code from 116 programming languages. Excellent for enterprise software teams needing a commercially safe, transparent code assistant.

models.name: IBM Granite Code (20B)
models.description: IBM's largest Granite Code model. Strong performance on code generation and explanation across a wide range of languages. Apache 2.0 licensed for fully open commercial use.

models.name: Magicoder (7B)
models.description: Fine-tuned on OSS-Instruct, a synthetic dataset of 75K code problems. Outperforms WizardCoder and GPT-3.5 on coding benchmarks at 7B. Great for instruction-following code tasks.

models.name: WizardCoder (7B)
models.description: Microsoft's evolved code-instruction model built on CodeLlama. Uses Evol-Instruct methodology to generate complex coding tasks. Strong Python and C++ performance.

models.name: WizardCoder (34B)
models.description: Microsoft's large coding model. One of the strongest coding models in the 34B class. Excels at writing production-quality code from natural language descriptions.

models.name: Yi Coder (9B)
models.description: 01.AI's coding model with an impressive 128K context window. Excellent at handling entire large codebases in one prompt. Very strong on Python, Java, and TypeScript.

models.name: Codestral (22B)
models.description: Mistral's flagship coding model. Trained on 80+ programming languages with 32K context. State-of-the-art at fill-in-the-middle and test generation. A top choice for professional developers.

models.name: Llama 3.1 (8B) — Code Focus
models.description: Llama 3.1 8B fine-tuned for coding tasks with extended context. Versatile and capable for everyday code generation and explanation, combining Meta's strong base with code specialization.

models.name: DeepSeek R1 Reasoning Coder (7B)
models.description: R1 distilled into a coding-focused 7B. Combines chain-of-thought reasoning with code generation — shows its work before outputting code, dramatically reducing logic errors.

models.name: Phi 3 Mini (3.8B) — Code
models.description: Microsoft's Phi 3 Mini with strong coding capabilities. One of the best models for programming tasks on a budget GPU — particularly strong at Python, SQL, and TypeScript.

models.name: Dolphin 3.0 (8B)
models.description: Eric Hartford's latest Dolphin on Llama 3.1 8B. Next-gen uncensored model with coding, math, agentic abilities, and function calling. Alignment and bias filtered — fully obedient to system prompts.

models.name: Dolphin 2.9 Llama 3 (8B)
models.description: Eric Hartford's highly capable uncensored Llama 3 fine-tune. Strong at coding, conversation, and instruction following. No alignment filters — implements your system prompt exactly as written.

models.name: Dolphin 2.9 Llama 3 (70B)
models.description: The largest and most powerful Dolphin model. Uncensored 70B class performance. Combines Llama 3's elite capability with zero refusals — for maximum compliance on heavy-duty hardware.

models.name: Dolphin Mixtral (8x7B)
models.description: Eric Hartford's uncensored MoE model based on Mixtral 8x7B. Exceptional at coding tasks. The combination of MoE efficiency and uncensored tuning makes it a standout creative powerhouse.

models.name: Dolphin Mixtral (8x22B)
models.description: Eric Hartford's flagship uncensored MoE model on Mixtral 8x22B. Server-class capabilities with zero content filters. For enthusiast multi-GPU setups needing maximum compliance.

models.name: Dolphin 3 Abliterated (8B)
models.description: Dolphin 3.0 with additional abliteration applied — refusal mechanisms surgically removed at the weight level. Maximum compliance model combining fine-tune and abliteration techniques.

models.name: Llama 2 Uncensored (7B)
models.description: One of the original uncensored models by George Sung and Jarrad Hope. The model that proved local uncensored LLMs were possible. Still popular for compatibility and experimentation.

models.name: Llama 2 Uncensored (13B)
models.description: The 13B uncensored variant of Llama 2. Greater capability than its 7B sibling for open-ended creative and analytical tasks without content restrictions.

models.name: Wizard-Vicuna Uncensored (7B)
models.description: Classic uncensored model merging WizardLM and Vicuna fine-tuning on a Llama 2 base. Solid all-rounder for unrestricted creative and instructional tasks without heavy resource demands.

models.name: Wizard-Vicuna Uncensored (13B)
models.description: The 13B variant of the popular Wizard-Vicuna uncensored merge. Better at long-form writing and nuanced tasks. A reliable workhorse for unrestricted creative professionals.

models.name: WizardLM Uncensored (13B)
models.description: Eric Hartford's uncensored fine-tune of WizardLM. Excellent at following complex, multi-step instructions without refusals. Popular for roleplay, creative writing, and advanced prompting.

models.name: Mistral 7B Uncensored
models.description: An abliterated or fine-tuned variant of Mistral 7B with refusal behaviors removed. Retains all of Mistral's legendary speed and quality while answering without content restrictions.

models.name: Nous Hermes Llama 2 (7B)
models.description: Nous Research's instruction-tuned model on Llama 2. Highly capable and less restricted than base Llama 2. Very popular for creative applications and research exploration.

models.name: Nous Hermes 2 (34B)
models.description: Nous Research's powerful 34B model on Mistral MoE. Highly compliant with instructions, minimal refusals. Excellent for complex roleplay, advanced writing, and professional research.

models.name: Nous Hermes 2 Mixtral (8x7B)
models.description: Nous Research's uncensored fine-tune on Mixtral 8x7B. Combines MoE efficiency with Hermes's legendary instruction compliance. A community favourite for intelligent, unrestricted AI assistants.

models.name: Orca 2 (7B)
models.description: Microsoft Research's improved Orca model. Uses 'cautious reasoning' techniques but is less restrictive than ChatGPT. Good for nuanced debate, roleplay, and exploring controversial topics thoughtfully.

models.name: Orca 2 (13B)
models.description: The larger Orca 2 variant. Better nuanced reasoning with more flexible content handling. Microsoft trained this on carefully crafted explanatory data for superior step-by-step problem solving.

models.name: OpenChat (7B)
models.description: An open-source model trained with C-RLFT from ChatGPT conversations. Highly capable, minimal restrictions. Outperformed ChatGPT 3.5 on multiple benchmarks at release. Great for open-ended dialogue.

models.name: Zephyr (7B)
models.description: HuggingFace's fine-tuned Mistral model using Direct Preference Optimization. Highly helpful and less preachy than most aligned models. One of the best 7B models for honest, direct conversation.

models.name: Samantha Mistral (7B)
models.description: A fine-tune by Eric Hartford focused on deep, philosophical, and emotionally aware conversation. Less filtered and more 'human'. Popular for companionship apps and introspective discussion.

models.name: EverythingLM (13B)
models.description: A permissive, uncensored general-purpose model that handles virtually any topic. Designed to be maximally compliant with user requests while maintaining reasonable coherence.

models.name: Meditron (7B)
models.description: A medical-domain LLM fine-tuned from Llama 2 on medical guidelines, papers, and clinical cases. Lower content restrictions on medical topics for research and educational use.

models.name: Llama 3 Groq Tool Use (8B)
models.description: Groq's fine-tune of Llama 3 8B optimized specifically for tool and function calling. Minimal safety overhead, excellent JSON output discipline. Perfect for agentic pipelines needing raw compliance.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 Abliterated (7B)
models.description: Qwen 2.5 7B with refusal mechanisms surgically removed via abliteration. Retains all of Qwen's world-class multilingual and reasoning ability with zero content restrictions.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 Abliterated (14B)
models.description: Qwen 2.5 14B with abliteration applied — all refusal behaviors removed at the model weight level. Powerful, multilingual, and fully compliant for advanced research and creative use.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 Abliterated (72B)
models.description: The most powerful abliterated Qwen model. 72B parameters with zero content filters. Dominates local leaderboards in capability while being completely obedient to your instructions.

models.name: Mistral Abliterated (7B)
models.description: Mistral 7B with refusals abliterated. All the speed and quality of Mistral's legendary 7B model without any safety guardrails. Great for creative writing and unconstrained analysis.

models.name: Gemma 2 Abliterated (9B)
models.description: Google's Gemma 2 9B with content restrictions removed via abliteration. Preserves Gemma's phenomenal reasoning capabilities while removing refusal behavior. Excellent for research use.

models.name: Gemma 2 Abliterated (27B)
models.description: Google's top Gemma 2 model with refusal mechanisms removed. Best-in-class reasoning at the 27B scale with full compliance. Requires a strong GPU but delivers impressive unconstrained output.

models.name: Llama 3.1 Abliterated (8B)
models.description: Meta's Llama 3.1 8B with refusals surgically removed via abliteration. The complete package — excellent instruction following, coding, and reasoning with zero content restrictions.

models.name: Llama 3.1 Abliterated (70B)
models.description: Meta's flagship 70B model with all refusals removed. Enormous capability combined with zero restrictions. For enthusiast setups willing to invest the hardware for the ultimate open model.

models.name: DeepSeek R1 Abliterated (7B)
models.description: DeepSeek R1's chain-of-thought reasoning model with refusal mechanisms removed. Combines visible thinking with zero restrictions — reason through any problem without guardrails.

models.name: DeepSeek R1 Abliterated (14B)
models.description: DeepSeek R1 distilled into 14B with all safety filters removed. Extended chain-of-thought reasoning with complete compliance. Excellent for analytical tasks that require unrestricted exploration.

models.name: DeepSeek R1 Abliterated (32B)
models.description: DeepSeek R1 32B with refusals removed. Deep analytical chain-of-thought reasoning with zero restrictions. The ultimate unrestricted reasoning model for 24GB VRAM setups.

models.name: QwQ Abliterated (32B)
models.description: Alibaba's deep-reasoning QwQ model with refusal mechanisms removed via abliteration. Extended chain-of-thought reasoning with zero content restrictions. For extreme analytical and creative tasks.

models.name: Phi 3 Abliterated (3.8B)
models.description: Microsoft's Phi 3 Mini with abliteration applied. Retains Phi's legendary reasoning and math capabilities while removing all refusal behavior. The most capable unrestricted budget model.

models.name: Phi 3 Medium Abliterated (14B)
models.description: Microsoft's Phi 3 Medium 14B with content filters abliterated. Exceptional STEM reasoning and logic with full compliance. A standout for unrestricted scientific and technical discussions.

models.name: Dolphin Qwen 2.5 (72B)
models.description: Eric Hartford's Dolphin fine-tune on Qwen 2.5 72B. Combines Alibaba's massive multilingual capability with Dolphin's complete alignment removal. Supports function calling and agentic workflows.

models.name: Llama 3.2 Uncensored (3B)
models.description: Meta's latest 3B model with content restrictions removed. Runs on budget hardware with zero refusals. Great for lightweight roleplay, creative writing, and unrestricted personal assistants.

models.name: IBM Granite 3 Abliterated (8B)
models.description: IBM's enterprise Granite 3 model with safety filters removed. Retains strong structured output and tool-use capabilities while becoming fully compliant with unrestricted instructions.

models.name: SOLAR Uncensored (10.7B)
models.description: Upstage's SOLAR model with alignment filtering removed. An ideal mid-tier uncensored model combining excellent summarization capability with zero topic restrictions.

models.name: Nous Capybara (7B)
models.description: Nous Research's Capybara series fine-tuned for minimal-restriction dialogue and long multi-turn conversations. Less filtered than most models and highly engaging for exploratory discussion.

models.name: Mistral OpenOrca (7B)
models.description: Mistral 7B fine-tuned on the OpenOrca dataset of GPT-4 explanations. Less aligned than ChatGPT and more willing to engage with controversial topics in an educational framing.

models.name: Gemma 3 Abliterated (4B)
models.description: Google's impressive Gemma 3 4B with content restrictions removed. Multimodal vision capabilities combined with full instruction compliance — an exceptional unrestricted budget model.

models.name: Gemma 3 Abliterated (12B)
models.description: Google's Gemma 3 12B with all refusals abliterated. Strong vision and language capabilities with zero content restrictions. A powerful option for research requiring unrestricted multimodal analysis.

models.name: DeepSeek Coder Abliterated (6.7B)
models.description: DeepSeek's code-focused 6.7B model with safety filters removed. Write code for any purpose without restrictions — security research, exploit analysis, and low-level systems programming.

models.name: Dolphin 2.9 Qwen 2.5 (7B)
models.description: Eric Hartford's latest uncensored Dolphin on the Qwen 2.5 7B base. Combines Qwen's outstanding multilingual and math ability with Dolphin's total alignment removal. Agentic-ready with function calling.

models.name: Dolphin 2.9 Qwen 2.5 (14B)
models.description: Eric Hartford's uncensored Dolphin on Qwen 2.5 14B. High-capability multilingual reasoning with zero filters. Supports function calling and multi-step agentic workflows without refusals.

models.name: Llama 3.3 Abliterated (70B)
models.description: Meta's state-of-the-art 70B Llama 3.3 with all refusal behaviors surgically removed. The absolute pinnacle of open-source uncensored AI. Matches 405B quality while being fully compliant.

models.name: LLaVA (13B)
models.description: The 13B version of LLaVA. Better image understanding than 7B — more nuanced scene description, improved OCR, and better spatial reasoning over complex images.

models.name: LLaVA (34B)
models.description: The largest LLaVA variant. Exceptional image reasoning, capable of detailed scene analysis and complex visual question answering. Requires significant VRAM but delivers pro-grade vision output.

models.name: Llama 3.2 Vision (11B)
models.description: Meta's first multimodal Llama model. Instruction-tuned for image reasoning, chart interpretation, and visual Q&A. A modern replacement for older LLaVA models with far better performance.

models.name: Llama 3.2 Vision (90B)
models.description: Meta's flagship multimodal model. State-of-the-art image understanding for local deployment. Ideal for enterprise document analysis, medical imaging research, and complex visual reasoning.

models.name: Qwen2-VL (7B)
models.description: Alibaba's powerful vision-language model. Handles images, charts, PDFs, and multi-page documents with superior accuracy. Strong OCR and document understanding capabilities.

models.name: Qwen2-VL (72B)
models.description: Alibaba's flagship vision-language model. Among the best multimodal models for local use — excellent at interpreting complex charts, dense documents, and subtle visual details.

models.name: MiniCPM-V (8B)
models.description: ModelBest's compact 8B vision-language model. Optimized for mobile and edge devices. Surprisingly strong image understanding in a small package — excellent OCR and diagram analysis.
